title: How to pay - Required tax instalments for individuals - Canada.ca
content:
Required tax instalments for individuals
- Choose a payment method
- Confirm your instalment payment is received

## Choose a payment method
You can pay your tax instalments online, in person, or by mail. There are several payment options with different processing times for each.
Pay your instalments using an online payment option.
Options to pay online
You will need your instalment remittance voucher (form INNS3) to ensure your payment is applied to the correct account.
Your remittance voucher is included in your instalment reminder package the CRA mails to you, unless you pay instalments by pre-authorized debit.
Choose your payment method :
Answer a few questions about your situation to find the right payment option for the type of payment you are making .
Get a remittance voucher :
- Sign in to your CRA account or Represent a client
- download a fillable remittance voucher
- call the CRA's automated service at 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281 , and select option 2
If your payment is not honoured, the CRA will charge a fee.

## Confirm your instalment payment is received
When you make a tax instalment payment, the CRA will not send you a receipt right away.
Keep proof of your instalment payment until you either:
- confirm the payment has been applied in CRA My Account
- receive your instalment payment summary (form INNS2) in February
- Confirm your payment in CRA My Account Before checking if your payment has been processed, allow: 3 business days for online payments 10 business days for payments by cheque or money order Then Sign in to your CRA account to view your payment details under "Accounts and payments".

### Confirm your payment in CRA My Account
Before checking if your payment has been processed, allow:
- 3 business days for online payments
- 10 business days for payments by cheque or money order
Then Sign in to your CRA account to view your payment details under "Accounts and payments".
- Resolve an issue with a payment If your payment to the CRA hasn't been applied as you expected, you can resolve the issue online or by phone: Online Sign in to your CRA account Under Accounts and payments , select 'Transfer payment(s)' and follow the steps to apply the payment correctly If 'Transfer payment(s)' does not appear, call the CRA to resolve your payment issue (see below). By Phone Call the CRA for issues with payments you can't resolve online: Canadian residents: 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281 Non-residents Canada or U.S.: 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281 Outside Canada or U.S.: 1-613-940-8495 (opens up phone application) 1-613-940-8495 To verify your identity, you will need to provide additional information, such as details from an assessed tax return.

### Resolve an issue with a payment
If your payment to the CRA hasn't been applied as you expected, you can resolve the issue online or by phone:
- Sign in to your CRA account
- Under Accounts and payments , select 'Transfer payment(s)' and follow the steps to apply the payment correctly If 'Transfer payment(s)' does not appear, call the CRA to resolve your payment issue (see below).
Call the CRA for issues with payments you can't resolve online:
- Canadian residents: 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281
- Non-residents Canada or U.S.: 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281 Outside Canada or U.S.: 1-613-940-8495 (opens up phone application) 1-613-940-8495
- Canada or U.S.: 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281
- Outside Canada or U.S.: 1-613-940-8495 (opens up phone application) 1-613-940-8495
To verify your identity, you will need to provide additional information, such as details from an assessed tax return.
If you paid more than you should have for a tax instalment payment, you can ask for a refund by:
- sending a letter to your tax centre
- calling 1-800-959-8281 (opens up phone application) 1-800-959-8281
The CRA will review the matter on a case-by-case basis.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
